fsm_model.description: Complete FSM for Joint Fabric Administrator Cluster including Joint Commissioning, ACL Management, Commissioning Window Control, and Anchor Transfer operations

states.name: Idle
states.description: Initial state where no commissioning or administrative operations are in progress

states.name: AwaitingVIDVerification
states.description: Received ICACCSRRequest but VID verification has not been completed

states.name: CSRRequested
states.description: ICACCSRRequest received and validated, ready to generate CSR

states.name: CSRGenerated
states.description: CSR has been generated and sent in ICACCSRResponse, awaiting AddICAC

states.name: ValidatingICAC
states.description: AddICAC received, performing three validation checks

states.name: ICACAccepted
states.description: ICAC validation succeeded, certificate installed, ready to complete commissioning

states.name: ICACRejected_InvalidPublicKey
states.description: ICAC validation failed due to public key mismatch

states.name: ICACRejected_InvalidICAC
states.description: ICAC validation failed due to chain validation or DN encoding failure

states.name: ErrorState_NoFailSafe
states.description: Command received without armed fail-safe context

states.name: ErrorState_VIDNotVerified
states.description: ICACCSRRequest received without VID verification

states.name: ErrorState_InvalidSession
states.description: Command received over non-CASE session

states.name: ErrorState_ConstraintViolation
states.description: ICACCSRRequest received when prior AddICAC already executed in fail-safe period

states.name: ErrorState_Busy
states.description: Commissioning operation cannot proceed due to another operation in progress

states.name: CommissioningComplete
states.description: Joint commissioning successfully completed, ICAC installed and operational

states.name: ACL_Active
states.description: ACL entry is active and enforced on the node

states.name: ACL_DeletePending
states.description: ACL removal initiated, status updated to DeletePending, attempting removal from node

states.name: ACL_Removed
states.description: ACL successfully removed from both node and ACL list

states.name: ACL_CommitFailed
states.description: ACL removal failed, status set to CommitFailed with error code for recovery

states.name: ACL_NodeNotFound
states.description: RemoveACLFromNode attempted on non-existent node

states.name: CommissioningWindow_Closed
states.description: No commissioning window is currently open

states.name: CommissioningWindow_Open
states.description: Commissioning window is open and accepting commissioning attempts

states.name: CommissioningWindow_Expired
states.description: Commissioning window timer has expired, window automatically closed

states.name: CommissioningWindow_Error_NullFabricIndex
states.description: OpenJointCommissioningWindow failed due to null AdministratorFabricIndex

states.name: CommissioningWindow_Error_PAKEParams
states.description: OpenJointCommissioningWindow failed due to invalid PAKE parameters

states.name: AnchorTransfer_Idle
states.description: No anchor transfer operation in progress, current node is active anchor

states.name: AnchorTransfer_RequestReceived
states.description: Transfer request received, checking preconditions

states.name: AnchorTransfer_InProgress
states.description: Anchor transfer is in progress, user consent obtained and datastore idle

states.name: AnchorTransfer_Complete
states.description: Anchor transfer completed, anchor role transferred to new administrator

states.name: AnchorTransfer_Rejected_NoConsent
states.description: Anchor transfer rejected due to lack of user consent

states.name: AnchorTransfer_Rejected_DatastoreBusy
states.description: Anchor transfer rejected due to ongoing datastore operations

states.name: EndpointAnnounced
states.description: Joint Fabric Administrator Cluster endpoint has been announced to client

transitions.guard_condition: case_session_active == true && fail_safe_armed == true && vid_verified == true && prior_AddICAC_executed == false
transitions.description: All preconditions met for ICACCSRRequest, proceed to generate CSR
ICACCSRRequest must be over CASE session
ICACCSRRequest requires armed fail-safe context

transitions.guard_condition: case_session_active == true && fail_safe_armed == true && vid_verified == false
transitions.description: VID verification must be completed before ICACCSRRequest

transitions.guard_condition: case_session_active == true && fail_safe_armed == true && vid_verified == true && prior_AddICAC_executed == true
transitions.description: Cannot request CSR if AddICAC already executed in fail-safe period
CSR successfully generated, send ICACCSRResponse

transitions.guard_condition: case_session_active == true && fail_safe_armed == true && prior_AddICAC_executed == false
transitions.description: AddICAC received with valid preconditions, begin validation
AddICAC must be over CASE session
AddICAC requires armed fail-safe context

transitions.guard_condition: case_session_active == true && fail_safe_armed == true && prior_AddICAC_executed == true
transitions.description: Cannot execute AddICAC if already executed in fail-safe period

transitions.guard_condition: chain_validation_success == true && pubkey_match == true && dn_encoding_valid == true
transitions.description: All three validation checks passed, install ICAC and respond with OK
Chain validation failed using Crypto_VerifyChain
Public key in ICAC does not match CSR public key

transitions.guard_condition: chain_validation_success == true && pubkey_match == true && dn_encoding_valid == false
transitions.description: DN encoding rules validation failed
Commissioning successfully completed with fail-safe
Fail-safe expired, rollback all operations including installed ICAC
Return to idle after error acknowledged
Return to idle after error acknowledged
Return to idle after handling no fail-safe error
Return to idle after handling VID not verified error
Return to idle after handling invalid session error
Return to idle after handling constraint violation error
Return to idle when commissioning operation completes
RemoveACLFromNode: Node exists and ACL present, update status and initiate removal
Node does not exist for given NodeID, return NOT_FOUND
ACL already not in list, no action needed, stay in ACL_Active
ACL successfully removed from node, remove from ACL list and update status
ACL removal from node failed, set CommitFailed status and store error code
Node refresh handles failed ACL removal, retry and return to active
Error acknowledged, return to normal state

transitions.guard_condition: AdministratorFabricIndex != null && pake_params_valid == true && commissioning_in_progress == false
transitions.description: All parameters valid, open commissioning window and start timer
Cannot open window with null AdministratorFabricIndex
PAKE parameters validation failed
Cannot open window while commissioning in progress
Timer decrement while window open
Commissioning window timer expired, automatically close window
Reset expired window to closed state
Return to closed state after handling error
Return to closed state after handling PAKE error
Anchor transfer request received, begin checking preconditions
User consent obtained and datastore idle, proceed with transfer
User has not consented to anchor transfer
Datastore operations in progress, cannot transfer now
Transfer complete command received, finalize anchor transfer
Transfer finalized, return to idle (no longer anchor)
Return to idle after rejection due to no consent
Return to idle after rejection due to datastore busy
Announce endpoint holding Joint Fabric Administrator Cluster
Return to idle after announcement

functions.name: generate_PKCS10_CSR_DER_encoded
functions.description: Generate a DER-encoded PKCS #10 Certificate Signing Request containing the node's public key for ICAC cross-signing
functions.algorithm_detail: Generate PKCS#10 CSR per RFC 2986: create CertificationRequestInfo with subject DN, public key (from node's ECDSA keypair), and attributes; sign with node's private key using ECDSA-SHA256; encode entire structure in DER format per X.690. Output maximum 600 bytes.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T006 (CSRRequested -> CSRGenerated) to create CSR for ICACCSRResponse

functions.name: extract_public_key_from_csr
functions.description: Extract and store the public key from the generated CSR for later validation against received ICAC
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse DER-encoded CSR per PKCS#10 structure, navigate to subjectPKInfo field in CertificationRequestInfo, extract SubjectPublicKeyInfo containing algorithm identifier and public key bits. Return raw public key bytes for storage.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T006 to extract and store public key for comparison during ICAC validation

functions.name: Crypto_VerifyChain

parameters.name: certificates
parameters.description: Certificate chain to verify, starting with target ICAC and ending with Root CA

functions.return_type: boolean (true if valid, false if invalid)
functions.description: Verify the certificate chain from ICAC to Root CA using X.509 path validation
functions.algorithm_detail: Perform X.509 certificate path validation per RFC 5280: (1) Verify ICAC signature using Root CA public key and ECDSA-SHA256, (2) Check ICAC validity period (notBefore, notAfter), (3) Verify certificate extensions and constraints, (4) Confirm signature algorithm matches, (5) Validate issuer/subject DN chain. Return true if all checks pass, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ValidatingICAC state (transition T011, T012) to verify ICAC was signed by trusted Root CA of accessing fabric

functions.name: validate_public_key_match

parameters.name: stored_csr_public_key
parameters.description: The public key extracted from the CSR previously generated

functions.return_type: boolean (true if match, false if mismatch)
functions.description: Verify that the public key in the received ICAC matches the public key from the last ICACCSRResponse
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract SubjectPublicKeyInfo from ICAC certificate using Matter Certificate Encoding parser. Compare extracted public key bytes with stored_csr_public_key byte-by-byte. Return true if identical, false if any byte differs.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ValidatingICAC state (transition T011, T013) to ensure ICAC public key matches CSR public key

functions.name: validate_DN_encoding
functions.return_type: boolean (true if valid, false if invalid)
functions.description: Validate that Distinguished Name fields in ICAC follow Matter DN Encoding Rules
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse ICAC certificate subject and issuer DN fields. Verify compliance with Matter specification DN encoding rules: (1) Required RDN types present in correct order, (2) Each RDN properly encoded as UTF8String or PrintableString per Matter rules, (3) No prohibited characters or encoding violations, (4) DN length constraints satisfied. Return true if all rules satisfied, false if any violation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ValidatingICAC state (transition T011, T014) to validate ICAC DN encoding correctness

functions.name: send_ICACCSRResponse
functions.description: Send ICACCSRResponse command containing the generated CSR to the administrator
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct ICACCSRResponse message with field ID=0 containing csr value (max 600 bytes). Encode message per Matter protocol format. Transmit over active CASE session to requesting administrator. No encryption of CSR itself as CASE session provides channel security.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T006 to respond to ICACCSRRequest with generated CSR

functions.name: send_ICACResponse
functions.description: Send ICACResponse command with validation result to administrator
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct ICACResponse message with field ID=0 containing status_code enum value. Encode message per Matter protocol format. Transmit over active CASE session immediately upon validation completion. Status codes: 0=OK, 1=InvalidPublicKey, 2=InvalidICAC.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T011, T012, T013, T014 to respond to AddICAC with validation outcome

functions.name: send_error_response

parameters.name: error_code
parameters.description: The error status code to return

functions.description: Send error response with appropriate status code to command initiator
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct error response message containing error_code. Map to status codes: 0x02=Busy, 0x03=PAKEParameterError, 0x04=WindowNotOpen, 0x05=VIDNotVerified, 0x06=InvalidAdministratorFabricIndex, or standard codes INVALID_COMMAND, FAILSAFE_REQUIRED, CONSTRAINT_ERROR. Send over current session (CASE if available, else appropriate transport).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in multiple error state transitions to notify initiator of failure reason

functions.name: rollback_all_operations
functions.description: Rollback all operations performed within the fail-safe period, including installed ICAC
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through all state changes logged during fail-safe period. Reverse each operation: remove installed ICAC from certificate store, clear stored CSR and public key, reset VID verification flags, restore pre-commissioning state. Ensure atomic rollback - if any operation fails, system enters recovery mode. Called when fail-safe timer expires or explicit rollback requested.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T016 when fail-safe expires to revert all commissioning changes

functions.name: reset_commissioning_state
functions.description: Reset all commissioning-related state variables to initial values
functions.algorithm_detail: Set all commissioning flags to false: commissioning_in_progress=false, csr_generated=false, icac_installed=false, validation_in_progress=false. Clear stored CSR, public key, and ICAC values. Reset fail-safe state but preserve AdministratorFabricIndex. Do not modify fabric table or persistent configuration.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in error recovery transitions T017, T018 to clean up after commissioning failure

functions.name: reset_error_state
functions.description: Clear error state variables and return to operational state
functions.algorithm_detail: Clear error_status, error_message, and validation_failed flags. Reset any temporary state related to failed operation. Do not modify persistent state or ongoing operations. Prepare system to accept new commands.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in multiple error-to-idle transitions to clean up error state

functions.name: node_exists
functions.return_type: boolean (true if exists, false if not found)
functions.description: Check if Node Information Entry exists in datastore for given NodeID
functions.algorithm_detail: Query Joint Fabric Datastore Node Information registry for entry matching node_id. Check if node record present with valid status. Return true if node record found and active, false if no matching record or node marked as deleted.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T024, T025 guard conditions to verify node existence before ACL removal

functions.name: acl_list_includes
functions.return_type: boolean (true if present, false if absent)
functions.description: Check if ACL List for the node includes the specified ACL entry
functions.algorithm_detail: Query node's ACL List in datastore for entry matching acl_entry_list_id. Search through list of DatastoreACLEntryStruct records. Return true if matching list_id found with non-deleted status, false if not found or marked as deleted.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T024, T026 guard conditions to check ACL presence before removal

functions.name: initiate_acl_removal_from_node
functions.description: Begin process of removing ACL entry from the specified node
functions.algorithm_detail: Send ACL removal command to target node via secure channel. Command instructs node to remove ACL entry matching acl_entry_list_id from local access control list. Asynchronous operation - actual removal occurs on node, success/failure reported via callback events acl_removal_success or acl_removal_failure.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T024 action to start ACL removal after status set to DeletePending

functions.name: remove_acl_entry_from_list
functions.description: Remove ACL entry from the datastore ACL List after successful node removal
functions.algorithm_detail: Delete DatastoreACLEntryStruct record matching acl_entry_list_id from datastore ACL List. Update list indices if necessary. Mark entry as permanently removed. This is phase 2 of two-phase commit - only called after successful removal from node confirmed.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T027 after successful ACL removal from node to complete two-phase commit

functions.name: get_removal_error_code
functions.description: Retrieve the error code from failed ACL removal operation
functions.algorithm_detail: Query the last ACL removal operation result. Extract error code returned by node when removal failed. Error codes may include access denied, node unreachable, invalid ACL ID, or internal node error. Return error code for storage in FailureCode field.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T028 when ACL removal fails to capture error for later recovery

functions.name: retry_acl_removal
functions.description: Retry ACL removal during Node Refresh after previous failure
functions.algorithm_detail: During Node Refresh procedure, examine ACL entries with CommitFailed status. For each failed entry, re-attempt removal using stored acl_entry_list_id and node_id. If retry succeeds, transition to ACL_Removed. If retry fails again, update FailureCode with new error and remain in CommitFailed for next refresh cycle.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T029 during Node Refresh to handle previously failed ACL removals

functions.name: validate_pake_parameters

parameters.name: verifier
parameters.description: PAKE verifier, must be exactly 97 bytes

functions.return_type: boolean (true if all valid, false if any invalid)
functions.description: Validate all PAKE parameters for OpenJointCommissioningWindow command
functions.algorithm_detail: Check constraints: (1) verifier byte_length == 97, (2) discriminator <= 4095, (3) 1000 <= iterations <= 100000, (4) 16 <= salt byte_length <= 32. Additionally verify verifier format matches SPAKE2+ verifier structure. Return true only if all constraints satisfied, false if any violation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called as guard condition in transitions T031, T033 to validate commissioning window parameters

functions.name: get_pake_validation_error
functions.description: Get detailed reason for PAKE parameter validation failure
functions.algorithm_detail: Examine which PAKE constraint failed: verifier length != 97, discriminator > 4095, iterations out of range, salt length out of range, or verifier format invalid. Return descriptive string identifying specific failure for debugging.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T033 to populate validation_failure_reason for PAKE error reporting

functions.name: start_window_timer
functions.description: Start commissioning window timer with specified duration
functions.algorithm_detail: Initialize window_timer to duration value in seconds. Set window_timer_active=true. Start periodic timer that decrements window_timer by 1 every second. When window_timer reaches 0, generate timer_expired event to trigger window closure. Timer resolution is 1 second.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T031 when opening commissioning window to enforce timeout

functions.name: cleanup_window_parameters
functions.description: Clear all commissioning window parameters when window closes
functions.algorithm_detail: Securely erase sensitive window parameters: pake_verifier (97 bytes), salt (16-32 bytes). Clear discriminator and iterations. Set window_open=false, window_timer_active=false. Ensure PAKE verifier is zeroed to prevent key recovery. Prepare system to accept new OpenJointCommissioningWindow command.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T036 when window expires to clean up parameters

functions.name: reset_window_state
functions.description: Reset commissioning window state variables to initial closed state
functions.algorithm_detail: Set window_expired=false, window_open=false, window_timer=0, window_timer_active=false. Clear any residual window parameters. Reset state to allow new OpenJointCommissioningWindow command. Do not modify AdministratorFabricIndex.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T037 to reset window state after expiration acknowledged

functions.name: check_transfer_preconditions
functions.description: Check both preconditions for anchor transfer: user consent and datastore idle
functions.algorithm_detail: Check two conditions: (1) Query user consent mechanism to determine if user has explicitly consented to anchor transfer (implementation-specific UI/physical button), (2) Query datastore for ongoing operations - check if any write operations, ACL modifications, or node updates in progress. Set user_consent_given and datastore_busy flags based on results. Generate preconditions_checked event when complete.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T040 when transfer request received to check if transfer can proceed

functions.name: get_requester_node_id
functions.description: Extract node ID of the anchor transfer requester from current session
functions.algorithm_detail: Query active CASE session to extract peer node identifier. This identifies the candidate administrator requesting anchor transfer. Return node-id from session peer information.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T041 to identify candidate administrator for anchor role

functions.name: send_TransferAnchorResponse
functions.description: Send TransferAnchorResponse command with status to requester
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct TransferAnchorResponse message with field ID=0 containing status enum value. Status codes: 0=OK (transfer proceeding), 1=DatastoreBusy (rejected due to operations), 2=NoUserConsent (rejected due to missing consent). Encode per Matter protocol format and send over CASE session to requester.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T041, T042, T043 to respond to transfer request with outcome

functions.name: initiate_anchor_transfer
functions.description: Begin anchor transfer protocol to transfer anchor role to candidate administrator
functions.algorithm_detail: Execute anchor transfer protocol: (1) Prepare anchor credentials and Root CA keys for transfer, (2) Establish secure channel with candidate administrator, (3) Begin transferring anchor fabric data including Root CA private key, fabric configuration, and node registry, (4) Await TransferAnchorComplete confirmation. Transfer is asynchronous and may take significant time depending on fabric size.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T041 after preconditions satisfied to begin transfer protocol

functions.name: finalize_anchor_transfer
functions.description: Complete anchor transfer by relinquishing anchor role and handing off to new anchor
functions.algorithm_detail: Complete transfer: (1) Verify TransferAnchorComplete received from candidate, (2) Securely delete local anchor credentials including Root CA private key, (3) Update local fabric table to recognize new anchor administrator, (4) Update is_anchor flag to false, (5) Transition own role from anchor CA to regular administrator. Ensure cryptographic material deletion is irreversible.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T044 when TransferAnchorComplete received to finalize transfer

functions.name: reset_transfer_state
functions.description: Reset anchor transfer state variables after transfer completes or is rejected
functions.algorithm_detail: Clear transfer state: transfer_requested=false, transfer_in_progress=false, transfer_complete=false. Clear candidate_administrator and transfer_status. Reset user_consent_given=false to require new consent for future transfers. Do not modify is_anchor flag - that reflects actual anchor status.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T045, T046, T047 to clean up after transfer completion or rejection

functions.name: send_announcement_confirmation
functions.description: Send confirmation response to AnnounceJointFabricAdministrator command
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct success response message for AnnounceJointFabricAdministrator command. Encode per Matter protocol format. Send over session to confirm endpoint announcement received and processed. Client can now route administrative commands to announced endpoint.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T048 to acknowledge endpoint announcement

functions.name: no_operation
functions.description: No-operation placeholder for transitions that require no action
functions.algorithm_detail: Empty function that performs no operation. Used in stay-transitions where guard condition prevents state change but no action is needed. Maintains FSM consistency by providing explicit action even when none required.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T026 when ACL already not in list

functions.name: start_validation_sequence
functions.description: Initiate the three-step ICAC validation sequence
functions.algorithm_detail: Begin sequential validation: (1) Call Crypto_VerifyChain with [ICACValue, RootCACertificate], set chain_validation_complete flag, (2) Call validate_public_key_match with received ICAC and stored CSR public key, set pubkey_validation_complete flag, (3) Call validate_DN_encoding with received ICAC, set dn_validation_complete flag. When all three complete, generate validation_complete event with results.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T007 to start validation process in ValidatingICAC state

definitions.definition: Certificate Authenticated Session Establishment - a secure session protocol in Matter providing mutual authentication using X.509 certificates and establishing encrypted communication channel
definitions.security_relevance: All certificate operations must occur over CASE to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks and ensure only authenticated administrators can request or install certificates

definitions.definition: A timed transaction mechanism that allows atomic rollback of all operations if commissioning does not complete successfully within the fail-safe period
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents partial credential installation that could leave nodes in inconsistent security states; ensures all-or-nothing commissioning

definitions.definition: Fabric Table Vendor ID Verification Procedure - validates that the requesting peer's vendor is authorized to participate in the joint fabric based on vendor identifier
definitions.security_relevance: Enforces vendor-based access control to prevent unauthorized cross-vendor certificate issuance and fabric infiltration

definitions.definition: Intermediate Certificate Authority Certificate - certificate issued by one fabric's Root CA to another fabric's node, enabling cross-fabric authentication in joint fabric scenarios
definitions.security_relevance: Core credential for joint fabric trust; must be properly validated to prevent certificate injection attacks

definitions.definition: Certificate Signing Request (PKCS #10) - cryptographically signed request containing a public key and identity information, used to request certificate issuance
definitions.security_relevance: Proves ownership of private key corresponding to public key; public key binding check ensures certificate issued for correct key

definitions.definition: A fabric-scoped index (1-254) identifying which fabric in the Fabric Table is associated with the Joint Fabric; null if no association exists
definitions.security_relevance: Authorization token - commissioning window cannot open if null, preventing commissioning without clear fabric authority

definitions.definition: Transaction protocol where ACL removal first sets DeletePending status, attempts removal, then either completes with Removed status or fails with CommitFailed status
definitions.security_relevance: Ensures atomic ACL removal with recovery capability - prevents inconsistent access control states where ACL partially removed

definitions.definition: Password Authenticated Key Exchange parameters including verifier (SPAKE2+), salt, iterations, and discriminator used for commissioning authentication
definitions.security_relevance: Weak parameters enable offline dictionary attacks; constraints enforce minimum security levels for commissioning

definitions.definition: The node in a joint fabric that holds the Root Certificate Authority role and can issue certificates for the entire joint fabric ecosystem
definitions.security_relevance: Root of trust for entire joint fabric; anchor transfer is critical security operation requiring user consent

definitions.definition: Matter-specific certificate format based on X.509 but with optimized encoding and specific DN structure requirements
definitions.security_relevance: Ensures certificate format compatibility and prevents format confusion attacks between Matter and standard X.509 parsers

definitions.definition: Distinguished Name encoding requirements specific to Matter certificates, including required RDN types, encoding formats, and structure
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents DN parsing vulnerabilities and certificate matching bypasses through malformed DN fields

definitions.definition: Multi-step protocol for cross-signing certificates between fabrics: VID verification, CSR request/response, attestation, ICAC addition
definitions.security_relevance: Complete protocol ensures proper authentication and authorization before cross-fabric credentials issued

security_properties.description: All certificate operations (ICACCSRRequest, AddICAC) must occur over authenticated CASE session
All commissioning operations within fail-safe period are atomic - either all succeed or all rollback on expiration
VID verification must complete before any certificate operations permitted
ICAC must be verified using Crypto_VerifyChain against Root CA before acceptance
ICAC public key must match public key from CSR to prevent key substitution attacks
ICAC DN fields must comply with Matter DN encoding rules to prevent parsing attacks
Only one AddICAC command may successfully execute within a fail-safe period
ACL removal uses two-phase commit: DeletePending then either Removed or CommitFailed
Failed ACL removals store error code and are retried during subsequent Node Refresh
Commissioning window automatically closes when timer expires
Commissioning window cannot open if AdministratorFabricIndex is null
PAKE parameters must satisfy security constraints: verifier 97 bytes, discriminator max 4095, iterations 1000-100000, salt 16-32 bytes
Anchor transfer requires both user consent AND datastore idle before proceeding
Once anchor transfer completes, original anchor loses anchor role permanently and cannot reclaim without reverse transfer
Joint commissioning must follow strict command sequence: ICACCSRRequest → ICACCSRResponse → AddICAC → ICACResponse
Only one commissioning operation may be in progress at a time

assumptions: Node private key is securely stored and not extractable
ECDSA signature provides proof of private key ownership
CSR cannot be forged without private key
Root CA certificate is trusted and authentic
Certificate validity periods are correctly enforced
Public key storage is tamper-proof between CSR and ICAC validation
Public key extraction from both CSR and ICAC is deterministic
DN parser is free from buffer overflow vulnerabilities
DN encoding rules are strictly enforced per Matter specification
SPAKE2+ is secure against offline dictionary attacks with proper parameters
Verifier is generated from sufficiently random passcode
Iterations count provides adequate work factor

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: Certificate-based authentication with ECDHE key exchange
assumptions: CASE protocol provides secure mutual authentication
Session keys are not extractable or predicable
Man-in-the-middle attacks are prevented by certificate validation

security_assumptions.assumption: CASE protocol provides secure mutual authentication and prevents man-in-the-middle attacks
security_assumptions.if_violated: All certificate operations vulnerable to MITM; attacker can inject unauthorized certificates

security_assumptions.assumption: Crypto_VerifyChain correctly implements X.509 path validation and detects invalid signatures
security_assumptions.if_violated: Invalid ICAC certificates accepted; unauthorized CAs can issue certificates for fabric

security_assumptions.assumption: Fail-safe mechanism reliably rolls back all operations on timer expiration or system crash
security_assumptions.if_violated: Partial credentials persist; nodes left in inconsistent security states

security_assumptions.assumption: VID Verification Procedure correctly validates vendor identity and cannot be spoofed
security_assumptions.if_violated: Unauthorized vendors gain cross-fabric access; vendor-based isolation broken

security_assumptions.assumption: Private keys corresponding to certificates are securely stored and not extractable
security_assumptions.if_violated: Compromised private keys enable impersonation; all certificate-based security fails

security_assumptions.assumption: Public key storage is tamper-proof between CSR generation and ICAC validation
security_assumptions.if_violated: Public key binding check bypassed; attacker can substitute different public key

security_assumptions.assumption: User consent mechanism is tamper-proof and accurately reflects user intention
security_assumptions.if_violated: Anchor transfer without user knowledge; complete fabric takeover possible

security_assumptions.assumption: System time and event ordering can be reliably established
security_assumptions.if_violated: Race conditions in command sequencing; timing-based attacks possible

security_assumptions.assumption: Matter Certificate Encoding and DER parsers are free from buffer overflow vulnerabilities
security_assumptions.if_violated: Malformed certificates achieve code execution or bypass validation

security_assumptions.assumption: PAKE protocol with specified parameter constraints is secure against offline dictionary attacks
security_assumptions.if_violated: Commissioning credentials vulnerable to brute-force; unauthorized network access

security_assumptions.assumption: Datastore operations are correctly tracked and busy status accurately reflects ongoing operations
security_assumptions.if_violated: Anchor transfer during datastore operations creates split-brain with conflicting anchors

security_assumptions.assumption: Node Refresh mechanism reliably retries failed ACL removals
security_assumptions.if_violated: Failed ACL removals never recover; permanent access control inconsistencies
